message:
fix(reactor): TokenSlab::clear now invalidates stale tokens via generation bump

Previously, clear() called self.entries.clear() which deallocated all
slots and reset free_head.  This meant a token issued before clear()
could accidentally alias a slot allocated after clear(), since the
generation counter was lost with the old entries.

The new implementation walks the entries array in reverse (preserving
the free-list order so slot 0 is allocated first, matching fresh-slab
behavior) and transitions each entry to Vacant with its generation
incremented by one.  This guarantees:

- Stale tokens from before clear() fail the generation check on
  contains/get/get_mut/remove, even if their index is reused
- The slab length resets to zero while the backing storage is retained,
  avoiding unnecessary reallocation on the next insert cycle
- Free-list topology is identical to a freshly-constructed slab of the
  same capacity, so allocation order is deterministic

Add slab_clear_invalidates_stale_tokens test verifying that:
  1. A pre-clear token is rejected by contains()
  2. The same index slot is reused on next insert
  3. The fresh token has a different generation than the stale one
  4. get() with the stale token returns None even after slot reuse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
